description:
To ensure the logon failure delay controlled by /etc/login.defs is set properly, add or correct the FAIL_DELAY setting in /etc/login.defs to read as follows: 
FAIL_DELAY
rationale:
Increasing the time between a failed authentication attempt and re-prompting to
enter credentials helps to slow a single-threaded brute force attack.
ocil_clause: the value of "FAIL_DELAY" is not set to "<sub idref="var_accounts_fail_delay" />" or greater, or the line is commented out
ocil:
Verify Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 enforces a delay of at least seconds between console logon prompts following a failed logon attempt with the following command: 
$ sudo grep -i "FAIL_DELAY" /etc/login.defs
FAIL_DELAY
fixtext:
Configure the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 to enforce a delay of at least <sub idref="var_accounts_fail_delay" /> seconds between logon prompts following a failed console logon attempt.

Modify the "/etc/login.defs" file to set the "FAIL_DELAY" parameter to <sub idref="var_accounts_fail_delay" /> or greater:

FAIL_DELAY <sub idref="var_accounts_fail_delay" />
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must enforce a delay of at least four seconds between logon prompts following a failed logon attempt.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must enforce a delay of at least four seconds between logon prompts following a failed logon attempt.
stig.vuldiscussion:
Increasing the time between a failed authentication attempt and re-prompting to
enter credentials helps to slow a single-threaded brute force attack.
stig.checktext:
Verify Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 enforces a delay of at least four seconds between console logon prompts following a failed logon attempt with the following command:

$ grep -i fail_delay /etc/login.defs

FAIL_DELAY 4

If the value of "FAIL_DELAY" is not set to "4" or greater, or the line is commented out, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Configure the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 to enforce a delay of at least 4 seconds between logon prompts following a failed console logon attempt.

Modify the "/etc/login.defs" file to set the "FAIL_DELAY" parameter to 4 or greater:

FAIL_DELAY 4

title: Ensure the Logon Failure Delay is Set Correctly in login.defs
